title: Linux Terminal Intro
summary: Learn basic commands, files, folders, apt installs, SSH concepts, and why the terminal is useful.

title: How I Built LaunchShell.org
summary: See how LaunchShell was built with plain HTML, shared CSS, Git, GitHub, JSON, Cloudflare Pages, and simple project documentation.

title: Git and GitHub
summary: Learn commits, rollback, branches, GitHub repos, safe sharing, and why every student project should use version control.

title: GitHub Codespaces
summary: Use a browser-based Linux coding environment for class repos, terminal practice, Python, C++, Java, and databases.

title: AWS Free VPS Setup
summary: Create a small disposable cloud server, connect with SSH, update Linux, harden access, and test a web server.

title: Termius Mobile SSH
summary: Use Termius to connect to a cloud server from your phone, run safe checks, and trigger repeatable maintenance scripts.

title: What Is a VM?
summary: Learn what virtual machines are, why snapshots matter, and how local labs differ from cloud VMs.

title: What Is Hacking?
summary: Learn the builder mindset behind hacking, safe lab rules, hacker history, and how penetration testing fits today.

title: Visual Studio Code
summary: Use VS Code as a project workspace for files, terminal commands, Git, extensions, Python, Java, C++, and SSH.

title: Use Libby With Your Library
summary: Set up Libby with a public library card and use free ebooks and audiobooks as a practical learning resource.

title: Cloudflare for Student Sites
summary: Learn how GitHub, Cloudflare Pages, DNS, domains, HTTPS, proxying, security rules, access control, and Workers fit together.

title: Nmap Recon Guide
summary: Learn safe network discovery, service checks, output files, and basic recon habits for your own lab systems.

title: Wireshark Packet Analysis
summary: Use Wireshark as a packet microscope for lab traffic, captures, filters, and basic network evidence.

title: Advanced Wireshark
summary: Move from basic captures into cleaner packet evidence, deeper protocol review, and more careful troubleshooting.

title: What Is an API?
summary: Learn how web APIs work with requests, responses, JSON, status codes, keys, and small Python projects.

title: Arduino and ESP32 Overview
summary: Learn how microcontroller boards connect code, circuits, sensors, soldering, power, and networking.

title: Certification Roadmap
summary: Compare practical certification paths for networking, Linux, cloud, blue team, offensive security, and student budgets.

title: Build and Deploy a Web App
summary: Build and deploy a simple Flask app backed by JSON files on a cheap server with Gunicorn, Apache, systemd, DNS, HTTPS, logs, and backups.

title: T-Pot Honeynet
summary: Set up a disposable T-Pot honeynet, monitor dashboards, handle screenshots and evidence, summarize findings safely, and shut the lab down cleanly.

title: 8-Bit CPU
summary: Explore a from-scratch 8-bit CPU, Python assembler tooling, ROM image generation, and an Arduino Nano EEPROM programmer workflow.

title: Small Python Projects
summary: Start with a Diceware passphrase generator, then try file tools, log parsers, hash checkers, and simple automation scripts.

title: Tiny Terminal Weather App
summary: Build a small Python command-line weather app, call an API, read JSON, and print useful forecast details in the terminal.

title: Tiny Terminal Weather App 2.0
summary: Expand the weather script with a simple menu flow, cleaner choices, and a more useful terminal workflow.

title: JSON Book Recommendations
summary: Turn a private audiobook export into clean public JSON, then use it to render a static book recommendations page.

title: Raspberry Pi + Alfa Wi-Fi Sniffer
summary: Use a Raspberry Pi and Alfa Wi-Fi adapter to capture lab traffic, save packet evidence, and review it safely with Wireshark.

title: Solar ESP32 MQTT Sensor
summary: Build a solar-powered ESP32 sensor node that reads temperature and humidity, then publishes the data over MQTT to a local server.

title: Java Movie CLI
summary: Build a command-line Java movie tracking tool with menus, files, search, and small database-style habits.

title: Java Radix Sort for IP Addresses
summary: Sort IP addresses with a Java radix sort project that connects data structures, parsing, and network-style values.

title: Student Resource List
summary: A collection of free and low-cost tools students can use to build real projects, learn cloud computing, practice web development, explore cybersecurity, and document technical work.

title: Book Recommendations
summary: A curated Top 30 reading list for cybersecurity, Linux, networking, programming, AI, and systems thinking.

title: GitHub Student Developer Pack
summary: Use student credits, free tiers, developer tools, and outside learning platforms to build, back up, break safely, fix, and document real technical work.

title: GitHub
summary: GitHub teaches version control, commits, branches, project history, public documentation, private work-in-progress repos, and portfolio building.

title: GitHub Codespaces
summary: Codespaces gives students a Linux-based browser coding environment without needing a perfect local setup first.

title: GitHub Copilot Student
summary: Copilot can help explain code, suggest syntax, debug small mistakes, and speed up repetitive coding when students still read, test, and understand the result.

title: Visual Studio Code
summary: VS Code is practical, common, extensible, and works well with Git, SSH, Python, web development, Markdown, and class projects.

title: Cloudflare
summary: Cloudflare is useful for domains, DNS, Pages hosting, proxying, security rules, access control, and edge Workers.

title: AWS
summary: AWS gives students a real cloud server path for learning public IPs, EC2, SSH, security groups, Linux, budgets, and cloud cost awareness.

title: Termius
summary: Termius makes SSH easier to manage across devices and helps students practice remote Linux administration.

title: Hack The Box Academy
summary: Hack The Box Academy gives structured cybersecurity lessons and labs for students who want guided practice instead of random hacking videos.

title: Microsoft Azure Credit
summary: Azure helps students learn cloud services beyond a single server, including app hosting, databases, identity, and monitoring.

title: Cisco Networking Academy
summary: Cisco NetAcad is useful for networking fundamentals, packet tracing, IP addressing, switching, routing, and certification prep.

title: MongoDB Atlas
summary: Hosted databases let students build real app backends without maintaining database infrastructure first.

title: Arduino Cloud and Adafruit IO
summary: These tools connect software with real sensors, dashboards, devices, and electronics projects.

title: GitHub Pages
summary: GitHub Pages hosts static websites directly from a repository as a simple first publishing step.

title: DigitalOcean Credit
summary: A VPS is one of the best ways to learn Linux, SSH, firewalls, web servers, deployment, backups, and server troubleshooting.

title: Datadog, New Relic, and Sentry
summary: Monitoring tools teach logs, errors, uptime, performance, alerts, and how production systems are watched.

title: 1Password or Dashlane
summary: Password managers teach better credential handling, stronger passwords, and safer project secret storage.

title: FrontendMasters, Scrimba, Educative, and Boot.dev
summary: Structured courses help students fill gaps in web development, backend development, Python, JavaScript, and DevOps.

title: TryHackMe
summary: TryHackMe gives guided cybersecurity rooms and beginner-friendly labs for students who want structured practice.

title: Clerk and Appwrite
summary: These tools help students add login, users, storage, and backend features without building every system from scratch.

title: BrowserStack, LambdaTest, and Polypane
summary: These tools teach students that a website should work across browsers, screen sizes, and devices.

title: LocalStack
summary: LocalStack lets students emulate AWS-style services locally and learn cloud patterns safely.

title: Doppler
summary: Doppler teaches environment variables, API keys, and why secrets should not be hardcoded into projects.

title: Start Here: The LaunchShell Path
summary: A beginner-friendly route through Linux, GitHub, SSH, cloud servers, Python tools, and safe cybersecurity labs.

title: About LaunchShell
summary: The story behind LaunchShell as a second-career student portfolio, project journal, and practical learning resource.
